message:
docs(artifacts): update RaptorQ optimization records, Track-E closure assessment, and Track-F benchmark data

Four artifact updates from ongoing RaptorQ optimization work:

- raptorq_expected_loss_decision_contract_v1.json: Update generated_at
  timestamp to reflect latest contract evaluation cycle.

- raptorq_optimization_decision_records_v1.json: Add E5 command surface
  split documentation making explicit that the manifest-level comparator/
  rollback command_bundle (gf256_primitives) is intentionally distinct
  from the probe-level repro_command (gf256_dual_policy). Adds new
  command_surface_split section with notes explaining the rationale.
  Updates timeline with agent-mail coordination reference.

- raptorq_track_e_gf256_p95p99_highconf_v1.json: Add closure_assessment
  section documenting that E5 acceptance criterion #4 is NOT yet met.
  Auto proxy p95/p99 tails remain above baseline overall, with three of
  four dual-slice tails regressed versus baseline. Lists three blocking
  requirements: broader SIMD-active corpus, materially better auto tails,
  and recovered raw-sample percentile capture. Updates findings to note
  addmul_slices2_fused as the one operation that improves versus baseline.

- raptorq_track_f_wavefront_pipeline_v1.json: Fresh benchmark data from
  latest wavefront pipeline runs across all three scenarios (k=64/n=16384,
  k=96/n=16384, k=128/n=32768). Updated p50/p95/p99 timing values and
  delta_vs_sequential percentages reflecting current performance profile.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

-    "For this high-confidence single-scenario corpus, auto mode shows higher latency tails than baseline in both overall proxy p95/p99 and all four dual-slice operation tails.",
